title: First Solar Q1 2026: Revenue Hits $1.04 Bn, Module Sales Drive 24% Growth as Backlog Reaches 47.9 GW
text: First Solar achieved record first quarter 2026 revenue of $1.04 billion, representing 24% year-over-year growth, driven by strong module sales across U.S. and India markets. Total module sales reached 4.3 GW comprising 3.0 GW from U.S. facilities and 1.0 GW from India operations. The company's contracted backlog grew to 47.9 GW valued at $14.4 billion, providing multi-year revenue visibility through 2030. U.S. module sales averaged $0.34 per watt while India deliveries averaged $0.20 per watt, reflecting different market dynamics and customer segments. The company's U.S. facilities operated at 96% capacity utilization, indicating strong demand absorption and manufacturing efficiency. India operations contributed record sales volumes, driven by demand in the PMKUSUM segment where First Solar's technology compliance gives competitive advantages. The gross margin expanded to 47% from 41% in the prior year quarter, driven by higher Section 45X tax credit volumes and improved manufacturing efficiency. Net income of $347 million represented 65% year-over-year growth. Looking ahead, the company expects Q2 volumes of 3.4-4.0 GW with adjusted EBITDA of $400-500 million. The CURE technology launch at Perrysburg facility is ramping successfully and expected to unlock up to $600 million in additional technology adjuster revenue through 2028. The South Carolina finishing facility remains on track for H2 2026 commercial operations, adding 3.7 GW annual capacity.
